body.title: Ring memory toward hippocampal binding model
body.result: EpisodeBinder: groups ring entries into bound episodes with milieu/habit/tool context. Wired into _process_inner. replay_episodes + complete_episode for consolidation/recall. 14 tests.
body.description: Ring memory is a FIFO-50 of raw text. Biology: hippocampus binds input to existing knowledge structures — the context is the activation pattern, not the text. Current ring holds conversation + system messages + tool results in one undifferentiated buffer. Threads/trails/tails are patches around this absence. Direction: (1) Shrink ring to ~7 entries (conversation turns only, no system messages). (2) Each turn triggers activation that persists in tails. (3) Context passed to LLM built from currently-active nodes (TWM), not text buffer. (4) Sleep consolidation strengthens useful bindings, prunes noise. Ring becomes tiny phonological loop for literal recall; real context is graph activation state. Depends on T-twm-attentional-gating (conversation workspace must be clean first). Related: D010 (ring-memory), D099 (TWM global workspace).
